input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 9 (and earlier), Android used `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` to handle keyboard appearance, but this API was deprecated in Android 30+ and was removed/changed in .NET 10.

Let me provide guidance on the modern approach. In .NET 10 / Android 11+, the recommended approach is to use `WindowInsets` and the new `Edge-to-edge` behavior. The `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30.


For .NET 10, I should look at a few practical solutions: using `AdjustPan` as a simpler alternative, leveraging MAUI's built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` for automatic scroll behavior when the keyboard appears, or tapping into Android's `WindowInsetsCompat` API to manually handle keyboard visibility and adjust padding accordingly. The core issue is that edge-to-edge rendering in .NET 10 MAUI makes the traditional `AdjustResize` approach ineffective, so I need to handle keyboard insets directly using the `WindowInsets` API instead. I'll outline the practical solutions: either configure the manifest setting as a baseline, or implement proper inset handling to adjust padding when the IME appears.
